similarity_name: Integrated protein sequence and GO similarity
definition: Compute target-protein sequence similarity and Gene Ontology functional similarity, then combine them as an integrated similarity using noisy-or style composition, 1 - product(1 - component_similarity). Use this when target relatedness, rather than ligand structure alone, may drive apparent affinity performance.
example_usage: python composite_pairwise_similarity.py --inputs target_sequence_pairwise.csv target_go_pairwise.csv --method noisy_or --out pairwise_similarity.csv

citations.title: BASE: a web service for providing compound-protein binding affinity prediction datasets with reduced similarity bias

evidence.summary: The study calculates target-protein sequence similarity and functional similarity based on Gene Ontology biological process terms.
The paper combines sequence and functional similarities into an integrated metric and filters training samples by similarity to the test set.
Performance declines as target similarity cutoffs are lowered, motivating similarity-aware evaluation.

limitations: GO annotations can be incomplete or biased toward well-studied targets.
The integrated score can hide whether sequence or function is driving the curve.
Functional similarity should be recomputed for the ontology version and species in use.
quality_gates: Report sequence alignment method, GO namespace, semantic similarity method, and ontology version.
Plot sequence-only, GO-only, and integrated curves.
Control for training-set size changes when filtering by target similarity.
